articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

articles.headline: Dortmund captain Emre Can out for season with torn ACL
articles.description: Borussia Dortmund captain Emre Can will miss the rest of the season with a torn ACL in his left knee.

images.name: Can Dortmund torn ACL_260228 [1296x729]
images.caption: Emre Can leaves the field during Dortmund's game against Bayern Munich after sustaining an injury.
images.alt: Emre Can leaves the field during Dortmund's game against Bayern Munich after sustaining an injury.

images.name: Bayern Munich wins 3-2 thriller over Dortmund
images.caption: Bayern Munich gets a late goal from Joshua Kimmich to beat Borussia Dortmund 3-2 in Der Klassiker.

keyEvents.text: Goal! FC Bayern München 1, TSG Hoffenheim 0. Leroy Sané (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Thomas Müller.

Goal! FC Bayern München 2, TSG Hoffenheim 0. Raphaël Guerreiro (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Harry Kane with a through ball.
Goal! FC Bayern München 3, TSG Hoffenheim 0. Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the top left corner.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Alexander Prass replaces Diadié Samassékou because of an injury.
First Half ends, FC Bayern München 3, TSG Hoffenheim 0.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Gift Orban replaces Erencan Yardimci.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Mathys Tel replaces Kingsley Coman.
Second Half begins FC Bayern München 3, TSG Hoffenheim 0.
Goal! FC Bayern München 4, TSG Hoffenheim 0. Leroy Sané (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from the right side of the six yard box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Mathys Tel with a cross.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Leon Goretzka replaces Dayot Upamecano.
Thomas Müller (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Serge Gnabry replaces Harry Kane.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Haris Tabakovic replaces Adam Hlozek.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Finn Becker replaces Tom Bischof.
Goal! FC Bayern München 5, TSG Hoffenheim 0. Serge Gnabry (FC Bayern München) right footed shot from very close range to the bottom right corner.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Michael Olise replaces Leroy Sané.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Pavel Kaderábek replaces Valentin Gendrey.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Jamal Musiala replaces Thomas Müller.
Pavel Kaderábek (TSG Hoffenheim) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second Half ends, FC Bayern München 5, TSG Hoffenheim 0.

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Bayern race into a 2-0 lead vs. Hoffenheim) 2025/01/15 ESHEET

article.story:
Bundesliga leaders Bayern Munich scored three times in 19 minutes in the first half to crush Hoffenheim 5-0 on Wednesday and restore their four-point advantage at the top of the standings.

The Bavarians did not need long to open their account with Leroy Sane drilling in for the lead from a Thomas Muller assist in the seventh minute.

Raphael Guerreiro doubled it after a one-two with Harry Kane five minutes later and they killed off the game with a 26th minute penalty by the league's top scorer, Kane, who bagged his 16th goal in the competition.

The England captain has now converted all 12 of the penalties he has taken in his Bundesliga career, the league's third best record.

The Bavarians picked up where they left off after the break with Sane scoring the second goal of the match three minutes after the restart before substitute Serge Gnabry tapped in their fifth in the 66th.

Hoffenheim were nowhere to be seen for 70 minutes with no single effort on target before Bayern keeper Manuel Neuer pulled off a stunning save to deny Finn Becker.

Despite cold and snowy conditions the hosts kept up the pressure and had more than half a dozen clear chances to add to their tally.

Bayern next host VfL Wolfsburg on Saturday before travelling to Feyenoord in a week for the Champions League.

Hoffenheim, who have won none of their last six league games and have now lost three on the trot, are 16th, in the relegation playoff spot.

The league leaders are on 42 points, four ahead of champions Bayer Leverkusen, who beat Mainz 1-0 on Tuesday.
